text: [@image:#1:Screenshot_20260303_101328_YouTube]Create a video game dashboard with individual pages for each of these items, so that we can see how we would navigate through each screen, like a video game in order to deeply understand all the editors and pages available to edit these characters
I'll analyze the reference image first to understand what elements need to be organized into a video game dashboard interface.

artifact.description:
The image displays a list-based interface with distinct items.

1.  **Header Section:** Contains global contextual information and potential navigation points:
    *   `mistercrunch`: User/Account identifier.
    *   `Merge pull request #3...`: Current active task/event.
    *   `97c2d0e`, `4 days ago`, `10 Commits`: Versioning/history information.

2.  **Main Content Area:** A scrollable list of individual items. Each item includes:
    *   **Icon:** Denoting item type (folder or document).
    *   **Name:** Unique identifier (e.g., `memory/agor-state`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`).
    *   **Description/Status:** Last commit message.
    *   **Timestamp:** Time since last activity.
    *   `CLAUDE.md` is currently highlighted, indicating selection.

3.  **Footer Section:** A preview/editor indicator.
    *   `README` with an edit icon: Suggests a default text content editor/viewer for selected items.

**Navigation and Editor Structure:**

*   **Global Dashboard:** The entire view serves as a dashboard.
*   **Item Pages:** Each listed folder (`memory/agor-state`, `repos`, `skills`) and file (`AGENTS.md`, `BOARD.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, etc.) represents an "individual page" in the video game dashboard.
*   **Folder Pages:** Clicking a folder would navigate to a new list view showing its contents.
*   **File Editors:** Clicking a file (especially `.md` files) would open a dedicated "editor" or "viewer" for that specific item's content. The footer's `README` with an edit icon implies a text-based editor for file content.
*   **Character Editors:** Files like `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md` are prime candidates for individual "character editor" pages, where their `.md` content could define character attributes, abilities, or lore.
*   **Global Navigation Pages:** The header elements could link to overarching pages like "Account Settings" (mistercrunch), "Quest Log/Activities" (Merge pull request), or "Version History/Progress" (Commits).

text: Perfect! I can see this is a GitHub-style repository interface with various folders and markdown files. Now let me search for video game dashboard UI design references to create an engaging, game-like navigation system for these elements.

The overall aesthetic is whimsical and magical, with elements like glowing gems, ornate borders, and a backdrop of a mystical forest with mushrooms.

artifact.title: Images For Query: video game dashboard UI

text: Excellent! Now I have great references for game UI design. Let me create a comprehensive video game dashboard that transforms the repository structure into an interactive game-like navigation system. I'll design multiple screens showing how users would navigate through different sections.

artifact.prompt_text: A detailed video game character editor screen titled "AGENTS.md - CHARACTER EDITOR". Left side shows a 3D character preview window with rotating holographic figure. Right side has multiple editing tabs: "ATTRIBUTES", "ABILITIES", "IDENTITY", "BACKSTORY". The Attributes tab is open showing sliders for stats like Intelligence, Creativity, Memory, Processing Speed with glowing blue bars. Below are text fields for character description with markdown formatting toolbar. Top shows breadcrumb navigation "HUB > AGENTS > EDITOR". Bottom has action buttons "SAVE CHANGES", "REVERT", "EXPORT CONFIG" with glowing effects. Side panel shows version history "Last edited: 4 days ago, Commit: 97c2d0e". Dark sci-fi interface with cyan and purple accents, holographic displays, futuristic HUD elements.

A futuristic video game main dashboard interface with a dark purple and cyan color scheme. Center of screen shows "SYSTEM HUB" title with glowing effects. Six large hexagonal navigation tiles arranged in a grid: "MEMORY VAULT" (folder icon with brain symbol), "AGENTS EDITOR" (character silhouette icon), "CLAUDE PROFILE" (AI avatar icon), "REPOSITORIES" (database icon), "SKILLS MATRIX" (ability tree icon), and "BOARD COMMAND" (strategy map icon). Each tile has hover glow effects, progress bars underneath showing completion status, and small "LAST MODIFIED: 4 days ago" timestamps. Top header shows user "mistercrunch" with avatar, level indicator "LV 97", and "10 COMMITS" achievement badge. Bottom navigation bar with icons for Settings, History, and Exit. Sci-fi HUD elements, holographic effects, particle effects around tiles.

A video game file browser screen titled "MEMORY VAULT - AGOR STATE". Shows a 3D isometric view of floating data containers representing folders and files. Main folder "memory/agor-state" appears as a large glowing vault in center. Surrounding it are smaller file icons: "AGENTS.md" (character card icon), "BOARD.md" (tactical map icon), "CLAUDE.md" (AI core icon), "IDENTITY.md" (profile icon), "USER.md" (player icon), "README" (document icon). Each item has a holographic label, status indicator (green checkmark), and "MODIFIED: 4 days ago" timestamp. User can click any item to zoom in and open. Top shows navigation path "HUB > MEMORY > AGOR-STATE". Right sidebar shows item details panel with description "Merge pull request #3..." and metadata. Dark space-themed background with grid floor, neon blue and purple lighting, floating particles.

A video game character profile screen titled "CLAUDE.md - AI PROFILE EDITOR". Center shows a large holographic AI avatar with animated particles. Left panel has tabs: "CORE IDENTITY", "CAPABILITIES", "PERSONALITY MATRIX", "MEMORY BANKS". The Core Identity tab is active showing editable text fields with markdown content about Claude's identity and purpose. Right panel displays a skill tree diagram with interconnected nodes representing different abilities, some nodes glowing to show active skills. Bottom section has a timeline showing "EVOLUTION LOG" with commit history entries. Top navigation shows "HUB > PROFILES > CLAUDE". Action buttons at bottom: "UPDATE PROFILE", "RUN DIAGNOSTICS", "BACKUP STATE". Dark futuristic interface with glowing cyan text, purple accents, holographic displays, circuit board patterns in background.

A video game skill tree editor screen titled "SKILLS - ABILITY MATRIX". Center displays a large interconnected node network forming a tree structure. Each node is a circular icon representing a different skill (folders and files from the skills directory). Nodes are connected by glowing lines showing dependencies. Some nodes are fully lit (unlocked), others are dimmed (locked). Clicking a node opens a side panel on the right showing skill details: name, description from markdown file, requirements, and edit button. Left sidebar shows skill categories as collapsible lists. Top shows breadcrumb "HUB > SKILLS > MATRIX VIEW". Bottom has "SKILL POINTS AVAILABLE: 5" counter and buttons "SAVE CONFIGURATION", "RESET TREE", "IMPORT/EXPORT". Dark background with constellation-like pattern, glowing cyan and purple nodes, particle effects connecting nodes, futuristic HUD overlay.

text: Now let me create additional screens to show more navigation flows and editor pages:

artifact.prompt_text: A video game system map screen titled "NAVIGATION FLOW - SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE". Shows an interactive flowchart diagram with all screens connected by glowing lines. Center node is "MAIN HUB" with six branches leading to: "MEMORY VAULT" (which connects to file editors), "AGENTS EDITOR", "CLAUDE PROFILE", "REPOSITORIES", "SKILLS MATRIX", and "BOARD COMMAND". Each node is a small thumbnail preview of that screen. Arrows show navigation paths between screens. Some nodes have sub-branches showing deeper navigation levels. User can click any node to jump directly to that screen. Top shows "HUB > SYSTEM > NAVIGATION MAP". Legend in corner explains node types: Hub (purple), Editor (cyan), Viewer (green), Manager (amber). Dark background with constellation-style connecting lines, glowing nodes, zoom controls in corner, minimap showing full system structure, holographic interface style.

A video game database management screen titled "REPOS - DATA ARCHIVES". Shows a 3D grid of floating data cubes representing different repositories. Each cube has a label and glows with activity indicators. Main view shows "repos" folder expanded with sub-items displayed as smaller cubes orbiting around it. Left panel has filter options: "Sort by Date", "Filter by Type", "Search Archives". Right panel shows selected repository details: name, description, size, last modified date "4 days ago", commit count "10 commits", and quick action buttons. Top navigation "HUB > REPOSITORIES > ARCHIVES". Bottom shows storage capacity bar "CAPACITY: 67% USED" with glowing progress indicator. Dark space environment with floating data structures, blue and purple neon lighting, digital rain effects, holographic interface elements.

A video game strategic command board screen titled "BOARD.md - COMMAND CENTER". Center shows a large holographic tactical map with interconnected nodes representing different project areas. Top section has mission briefing panel with markdown text editor showing current objectives and tasks. Left sidebar displays "ACTIVE QUESTS" list with checkboxes and progress bars. Right sidebar shows "TEAM STATUS" with character avatars (Agents, Claude, User) and their current activity status. Bottom panel has "COMMIT LOG" showing recent changes as a timeline with icons. Navigation breadcrumb shows "HUB > BOARD > COMMAND". Action buttons: "NEW MISSION", "UPDATE OBJECTIVES", "SYNC STATUS". Dark military-tech interface with green holographic map, amber alert highlights, cyan text, tactical grid overlay, radar sweep effects.

A video game user profile management screen titled "USER.md - PLAYER PROFILE". Top section shows large player banner with username "mistercrunch", level "97", experience bar, and player avatar. Below are four main panels: "STATISTICS" showing commit count (10), projects active, last login (4 days ago) with graphs; "PREFERENCES" with toggleable settings and theme options; "ACTIVITY LOG" showing recent actions as a scrollable timeline; "LINKED PROFILES" showing connections to Agents, Claude, and other entities. Center has a markdown editor for user bio and notes. Navigation breadcrumb "HUB > USER > PROFILE". Bottom action bar with "SAVE PROFILE", "EXPORT DATA", "ACCOUNT SETTINGS" buttons. Dark gaming interface with cyan and purple gradients, stat cards with glowing borders, achievement badges, level-up effects, futuristic HUD elements.

A video game character identity editor screen titled "IDENTITY.md - PROFILE CONFIGURATION". Center-left shows a character card with customizable avatar, name field, and role selector. Right side has multiple collapsible sections: "CORE TRAITS" with personality sliders, "BACKGROUND STORY" with rich text editor, "RELATIONSHIPS" showing connection web to other characters, "ACHIEVEMENTS" displaying unlocked badges. Top tabs: "VISUAL", "PERSONALITY", "HISTORY", "CONNECTIONS". Navigation shows "HUB > PROFILES > IDENTITY". Bottom has version control panel showing "Current Version: 97c2d0e" with buttons "SAVE", "REVERT", "COMPARE VERSIONS". Left sidebar shows quick access to other profile files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, USER.md) as clickable icons. Dark RPG-style interface with gold and purple accents, ornate borders, glowing text fields, magical particle effects.

text:
Perfect! I've created a comprehensive video game dashboard system that transforms your repository structure into an immersive, navigable game interface. Here's what I've designed:

## 🎮 Complete Video Game Dashboard System

I've created **10 interconnected screens** that show how you would navigate through your system like a video game:

### **Core Navigation Screens:**

1. **Main Dashboard Hub** - Your central command center with hexagonal tiles for each major section (Memory Vault, Agents Editor, Claude Profile, Repositories, Skills Matrix, Board Command)

2. **Navigation Flow Diagram** - An interactive system map showing how all screens connect, allowing you to visualize and jump between any section

### **Character & Profile Editors:**

3. **Agents Character Editor** - Detailed editor with 3D character preview, attribute sliders, abilities tabs, and markdown editing capabilities

4. **Claude Profile Editor** - AI profile management with holographic avatar, skill tree, personality matrix, and evolution log

5. **Identity Profile Editor** - Character identity configuration with traits, background story, relationships web, and achievements

6. **User Profile Management** - Player profile with statistics, activity log, preferences, and linked profiles

### **Data & Content Management:**

7. **Memory Vault Navigation** - 3D isometric view of your file system with floating data containers for all .md files and folders

8. **Repository Database** - Data archives with 3D grid of floating cubes representing different repositories

9. **Skills Matrix Editor** - Interactive skill tree with interconnected nodes showing dependencies and unlock status

10. **Board Command Strategy** - Tactical command center with mission briefing, active quests, team status, and commit timeline

### **Key Features Across All Screens:**
- ✨ Futuristic sci-fi aesthetic with cyan/purple/amber color schemes
- 🎯 Breadcrumb navigation showing your current location
- 📊 Version control integration (commit history, timestamps)
- 🔄 Consistent user info display (mistercrunch, Level 97, 10 commits)
- 💫 Holographic effects, glowing elements, and particle systems
- 🎨 Each screen type has distinct visual identity while maintaining cohesive design

Each screen is designed to be fully functional, showing how you would navigate from the main hub to specific editors, manage your characters and profiles, and understand the complete system architecture!

```sug
<suggestion>
  <label>Create mobile version</label>
  <prompt>Create mobile/tablet optimized versions of these dashboard screens with touch-friendly controls</prompt>
</suggestion>
<suggestion>
  <label>Add dark/light themes</label>
  <prompt>Generate alternative color schemes for the dashboard - light mode, cyberpunk, and fantasy themes</prompt>
</suggestion>
<suggestion>
  <label>Animated transitions</label>
  <prompt>Create transition animation concepts showing how screens morph when navigating between sections</prompt>
</suggestion>
```
Create mobile/tablet optimized versions of these dashboard screens with touch-friendly controls
I'll create mobile and tablet optimized versions of the key dashboard screens with touch-friendly controls, larger tap targets, and responsive layouts.

artifact.prompt_text: Mobile phone vertical interface of the video game dashboard. Top header shows "SYSTEM HUB" title with hamburger menu icon on left and user avatar "mistercrunch LV 97" on right. Below are large touch-friendly card tiles stacked vertically: "MEMORY VAULT", "AGENTS EDITOR", "CLAUDE PROFILE", "REPOSITORIES", "SKILLS MATRIX", "BOARD COMMAND". Each card is rounded rectangle with large icon, title, subtitle showing "Modified: 4 days ago", and progress bar. Cards have generous padding and spacing for easy tapping. Bottom navigation bar with 4 large icons: Home, Search, Notifications, Profile. Swipe indicators between cards. Dark futuristic interface with cyan and purple gradients, glowing effects, optimized for thumb reach zones on mobile screen.

Mobile vertical profile screen for Claude AI. Top section has large holographic AI avatar (30% of screen) with animated particles, tap to view full screen. Below avatar is profile name "CLAUDE" with edit icon. Horizontal scrollable pill-shaped tabs: "IDENTITY", "CAPABILITIES", "PERSONALITY", "MEMORY". Content area shows Identity tab with expandable accordion sections, each with large touch targets and clear expand/collapse icons. Text content in readable mobile font size with good line spacing. Skill indicators shown as large circular progress rings. Bottom sticky toolbar with large buttons: "UPDATE", "BACKUP", "DIAGNOSTICS". Swipe up gesture reveals more details. Dark futuristic mobile interface with cyan text, purple accents, smooth animations, optimized for portrait orientation.

Mobile vertical interface for character editor. Top navigation bar with back arrow, "AGENTS EDITOR" title, and save icon. Below is compact 3D character preview taking 40% of screen with pinch-to-zoom and swipe-to-rotate gestures indicated. Horizontal scrollable tab bar with large touch targets: "ATTRIBUTES", "ABILITIES", "IDENTITY", "BACKSTORY". Content area shows Attributes tab with large touch-friendly sliders for Intelligence, Creativity, Memory, Processing Speed - each slider has big thumb control and value display. Collapsible sections with chevron icons. Large floating action button (FAB) at bottom right for "SAVE". Bottom sheet panel can be pulled up for more options. Dark interface optimized for one-handed mobile use, cyan and purple accents, smooth gradients.

Mobile vertical file browser interface titled "MEMORY VAULT". Top bar with back button, search icon, and filter icon. Breadcrumb navigation "HUB > MEMORY > AGOR-STATE" with horizontal scroll. Main area shows vertical list of large touch-friendly file cards: "AGENTS.md", "BOARD.md", "CLAUDE.md", "IDENTITY.md", "USER.md", "README". Each card has large icon on left, file name in bold, description text, timestamp "4 days ago", and chevron arrow on right. Cards have generous vertical padding (minimum 60px height) for easy thumb tapping. Swipe left on card reveals quick actions: Edit, Share, Delete. Pull-to-refresh indicator at top. Floating action button for "New File" at bottom right. Dark theme with glowing card borders, cyan highlights, optimized for mobile scrolling.

Tablet landscape interface of video game dashboard. Left sidebar (20% width) shows persistent navigation menu with large touch-friendly icons and labels: Memory Vault, Agents, Claude, Repos, Skills, Board. Top header spans full width with "SYSTEM HUB" title, user info "mistercrunch LV 97", and utility icons. Main content area (80% width) displays 2x3 grid of large dashboard cards with generous spacing. Each card shows icon, title, description, progress bar, and "Modified: 4 days ago". Cards have hover/tap states with glow effects. Bottom bar shows system stats and quick actions. Split-screen capable layout. Dark futuristic interface with cyan and purple gradients, optimized for tablet touch targets (minimum 44px), landscape orientation.

Mobile vertical interface for skills matrix. Top bar with back button, "SKILLS MATRIX" title, and view toggle (Tree/List). Main area shows simplified vertical skill tree optimized for mobile - large circular skill nodes (80px) arranged in vertical columns with connecting lines. Each node shows icon and name below. Tap node to select (glows), tap again to open bottom sheet with details. Pinch-to-zoom supported. Horizontal swipe between skill categories. Bottom sheet slides up showing selected skill: large icon, name, description, requirements, and large "UNLOCK" or "EDIT" button. Skill points displayed in top right corner. Floating action button for quick actions. Dark theme with glowing skill nodes, cyan for unlocked, gray for locked, purple connections, mobile-optimized vertical scrolling.

Tablet landscape interface for repository browser. Left sidebar (30% width) shows folder tree navigation with expandable folders, large touch-friendly chevrons and folder icons. Center area (45% width) displays grid view of repository items as large cards (2 columns) with icons, names, descriptions, and metadata. Cards have generous padding and spacing for touch. Right panel (25% width) shows selected item details: preview, properties, quick actions with large buttons. Top bar has breadcrumb navigation, search, view mode toggles (Grid/List/3D), and filter options. Bottom shows storage capacity bar. All interactive elements optimized for touch with minimum 44px targets. Dark space-themed interface with floating data cubes, blue and purple neon lighting, tablet landscape optimization.

Tablet landscape interface for board command center. Top section (30% height) shows tactical holographic map with touch-to-zoom and drag-to-pan controls, large interactive nodes. Left panel (35% width) displays "ACTIVE QUESTS" with large checkbox items, drag-to-reorder handles, and expandable quest details. Right panel (35% width) shows "TEAM STATUS" with character cards, status indicators, and tap-to-view-details. Bottom section (20% height) has horizontal scrolling commit timeline with large touch-friendly entries. Center has floating action button for "NEW MISSION". All interactive elements have minimum 44px touch targets. Top bar with navigation and action icons. Dark military-tech interface with green holographic map, amber highlights, cyan text, optimized for tablet landscape use.

Mobile vertical interface for board command. Top section shows mini tactical map (25% screen) with pinch-to-zoom, tap hotspots to view details. Below are vertically stacked sections with clear headers: "MISSION BRIEFING" (expandable card with markdown text), "ACTIVE QUESTS" (checklist with large checkboxes and swipe-to-complete), "TEAM STATUS" (horizontal scrolling character cards with avatars and status dots), "RECENT COMMITS" (vertical timeline with large touch-friendly entries). Each section is collapsible with chevron icons. Floating action button for "NEW MISSION". Bottom navigation bar. Pull-to-refresh at top. All touch targets minimum 60px height. Dark military-tech mobile interface with green accents, amber alerts, cyan text, optimized for one-handed portrait use.

Tablet landscape interface for skills matrix editor. Left sidebar (25% width) shows collapsible skill categories list with large touch targets. Center area (50% width) displays interactive skill tree with large circular nodes connected by glowing lines, optimized for touch - nodes are minimum 60px diameter for easy tapping. Pinch-to-zoom and pan gestures supported. Right panel (25% width) shows selected skill details with large text, scrollable description, and action buttons. Top bar has breadcrumb navigation and view mode toggles (Tree/List/Grid). Bottom toolbar with large buttons: "Save", "Reset", "Export". Skill points counter prominently displayed. Dark interface with constellation-style skill tree, cyan and purple nodes, tablet-optimized touch controls, landscape orientation.

text:
Perfect! I've created a complete set of mobile and tablet optimized versions of your video game dashboard. Here's what I've designed:

## 📱 Mobile Versions (Portrait 9:16)

**Touch-Optimized Features:**
- ✅ **Large tap targets** (minimum 60-80px height for easy thumb reach)
- ✅ **Vertical scrolling** layouts optimized for one-handed use
- ✅ **Bottom navigation bars** within thumb reach zones
- ✅ **Swipe gestures** (swipe-to-delete, swipe-between-tabs, pull-to-refresh)
- ✅ **Floating action buttons** (FAB) for primary actions
- ✅ **Bottom sheets** that slide up for additional options
- ✅ **Collapsible sections** to maximize screen space

**Mobile Screens Created:**
1. **Mobile Main Dashboard** - Vertical card stack with large touch-friendly tiles
2. **Mobile Agents Editor** - Compact character preview with large sliders and tabs
3. **Mobile Memory Vault** - List view with swipe actions and generous padding
4. **Mobile Claude Profile** - Large avatar with expandable accordion sections
5. **Mobile Skills Matrix** - Simplified vertical skill tree with bottom sheet details
6. **Mobile Board Command** - Stacked sections with collapsible headers

## 📱 Tablet Versions (Landscape 4:3)

**Tablet-Optimized Features:**
- ✅ **Split-screen layouts** with persistent sidebars
- ✅ **Multi-column grids** utilizing larger screen space
- ✅ **Dual-panel views** (list + details, tree + properties)
- ✅ **Touch targets** minimum 44px for comfortable tapping
- ✅ **Pinch-to-zoom** and pan gestures for complex views
- ✅ **Horizontal scrolling** sections where appropriate

**Tablet Screens Created:**
1. **Tablet Main Dashboard** - Sidebar navigation + 2x3 card grid
2. **Tablet Skills Matrix** - Three-panel layout (categories, tree, details)
3. **Tablet Board Command** - Split view with map, quests, and team status
4. **Tablet Repository Browser** - Folder tree + grid view + details panel

## 🎯 Key Mobile/Tablet UX Improvements:

### **Gesture Controls:**
- Swipe left/right to navigate between sections
- Pinch-to-zoom on maps and skill trees
- Pull-to-refresh for updating content
- Long-press for context menus
- Drag-to-reorder items

### **Responsive Adaptations:**
- **Mobile**: Single column, stacked content, bottom navigation
- **Tablet**: Multi-column, persistent sidebars, more information density
- **Both**: Large touch targets, clear visual hierarchy, smooth animations

### **Touch-Friendly Elements:**
- Large buttons and cards with generous padding
- Clear tap states with visual feedback
- Floating action buttons for primary actions
- Bottom sheets instead of modal dialogs
- Horizontal scrolling for secondary content

All screens maintain the futuristic sci-fi aesthetic while being fully optimized for touch interaction on mobile and tablet devices!

```sug
<suggestion>
  <label>Create gesture guide</label>
  <prompt>Create an interactive tutorial screen showing all the touch gestures and swipe controls for the mobile dashboard</prompt>
</suggestion>
<suggestion>
  <label>Dark mode variants</label>
  <prompt>Generate light mode and OLED dark mode versions of the mobile interfaces for different viewing conditions</prompt>
</suggestion>
<suggestion>
  <label>Accessibility version</label>
  <prompt>Create high-contrast, larger text versions of these screens optimized for accessibility</prompt>
</suggestion>
```
